question: Implement sliding window attention. Each token only attends to window_size tokens before it.
code_hint: Create mask: attn_weights[dist > window_size] = -inf before softmax
expected_output:
Window mask shape: torch.Size([5, 5])
Attended positions per row: 3
template:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

seq_len = 5
window_size = 3

# Create distance matrix
positions = torch.arange(seq_len)
distance = (positions.unsqueeze(0) - positions.unsqueeze(1)).abs()

# Sliding window mask
mask = distance <= window_size
print(f"Window mask shape: {mask.shape}")

# Apply to attention scores
logits = torch.randn(seq_len, seq_len)
masked_logits = logits.masked_fill(~mask, float('-inf'))
attn = F.softmax(masked_logits, dim=-1)
print(f"Attended positions per row: {mask[0].sum().item()}")
answer:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

seq_len = 5
window_size = 3

# Create distance matrix
positions = torch.arange(seq_len)
distance = (positions.unsqueeze(0) - positions.unsqueeze(1)).abs()

# Sliding window mask
mask = distance <= window_size
print(f"Window mask shape: {mask.shape}")

# Apply to attention scores
logits = torch.randn(seq_len, seq_len)
masked_logits = logits.masked_fill(~mask, float('-inf'))
attn = F.softmax(masked_logits, dim=-1)
print(f"Attended positions per row: {mask[0].sum().item()}")
